mentions.note: Semiconductor capital equipment; memory shortage will drive volumes; buyable on pullback after oil volatility passes.
Trade-down retail theme; financially challenged families moving to discount names; can buy some now and more after earnings.
Caller Jimmy T: not into enterprise software; last quarter not liked; sell.
Trade-down retail theme; reports tomorrow; Cramer has blessing to buy; financially challenged families using discount channels.
Trade-down retail theme; more adventuresome investor play; better for consumers watching budgets.
Caller Richard (NC): great natural gas portfolio, good merger, undervalued due to merger dynamics; Cramer: agree, but hesitate after big run; would buy on pullback.
Caller Bill (OH): owns FormFactor (semiconductor); Cramer: good technology, very expensive stock, but ride it through.
Eccentric specialty retailer uniting economic groups; caters to families avoiding high prices; Cramer blessing to buy.
Semiconductor capital equipment; less risky play on memory theme; ultimate winner as shortage persists; can buy on weakness.
Caller James (GA) holds position up 20%; Cramer: never mind taking profits, prefer hold; one of travel trust favorites; would buy more if hit.
Semiconductor capital equipment maker; less risky than memory stocks; memory shortage will drive business; buyable on pullback; currently too high.

2026 inflection year: $150M digital games investment, two major movie releases (Masters of the Universe), 3–6% growth guidance; CEO bought $1M stock; 18% buyback in 3 years signals undervaluation; balance sheet strong.
Caller Robert (CA) asked about volatility; Cramer: could embrace limited AI-driven restructuring (like Block), cut staff, boost shareholder value; potential inflection model.
Memory shortage theme; beneficiary of data-center buildout; buyable on pullback if oil rises.
Data-center champion; announced $2B investment in Nebius (AI cloud company); GTC event next week will showcase continued buildout; theme can shine once war ends.
Caller Mark (WI): semiconductor, burning cash but partnered with Nvidia; Cramer: good technology that should translate to earnings, not done so yet, ride it.

Q3 beat: 22% revenue growth, 21% EPS growth, $553B RPO; capex-light strategy via customer funding/prepayments eases balance-sheet fears; raised $30B debt, 10-month runway without equity dilution; OpenAI stability improves outlook.
Data-center backup power supplier crashed 29% on margin miss, but thesis intact; ramp costs temporary; trades 10x FY2026 earnings vs. Caterpillar 31x, Cummins 21x; Cramer: dislocation is entry point.
Trade-down retail theme; financial stress driving shift from full-price to discount retail.
Caller Greg (CA): Kathy Wood bought it; Cramer: big let-down, came on show, talked big game, didn't deliver; sell.
Caller KT (MA): called in January, stock down 30% since; Cramer: losing too much money; tough tape, not buying money losers; plenty of profitable stocks way down.
Memory shortage theme; can be bought on big move down due to oil; currently not recommended.
Caller Don (AL): AI stock; Jensen Huang had position (no longer major holder); meme stock losing money; Cramer: ixnay on meme stocks that lose money.
Memory shortage theme; currently too high; buyable on significant pullback if oil shock occurs.
Trade-down retail theme; HomeGoods, Marshalls, TJ Maxx divisions benefit as consumer shifts down.
Cosmetics retailer reports tomorrow; Cramer likes it; trade-down beneficiary; has blessing to buy some, then buy more after earnings.
Caller Tyler (OH): stock tripled in 12 months after new CEO/CFO; Q4 earnings beat, recent pullback normal; multiple ~21x; Cramer: did pretty good job, near-term weakness buyable.
Memory shortage persisting longer than expected (HP Enterprise confirmed); stock too high now; can buy on big move down if oil spikes to $120.

CEO Jack Dorsey announced 40% workforce cut (10K→6K) in favor of AI tools; stock rallied low 50s to high 60s; demonstrates AI productivity bet; may signal trend for big tech to adopt similar cost-cutting.
